key_points: The story critiques materialism and class consciousness
Mme Loisel’s tragedy results from her own choices
The necklace symbolises illusion and false pride
Human decisions, not fate, shape the characters’ destiny
The twist ending strengthens the moral impact
characters: Mme Loisel – A tragic figure whose pride and dissatisfaction lead to downfall
Mr Loisel – Moral anchor of the story, showing responsibility and endurance
Mme Forestier – Catalyst for irony through the fake necklace
important_events: Decision to hide the truth after losing the necklace
Years of physical and emotional transformation
literary_devices: Irony – Central device shaping plot and message
Realism – Ordinary characters facing realistic social struggles
Symbolism – Necklace as symbol of false status
moral_values: Honesty and humility are essential life values
exam_tips: Explain symbolism of the necklace clearly
Use themes to answer higher-order questions
